title: IntegrityRegister
description: JSON Schema for CXO integrity JSON-LD files. Each file is a register of words given by a CXO towards implementation of ASI Saga and Business Infinity, authored through Werner Erhard's ontological definition of integrity: Integrity = Honoring Your Word. Integrity is not a moral concept — it is the condition of wholeness and workability. When you are in integrity your word and your action are one. When you are out of integrity, things do not work. The register records each word given, by when it is to be honoured, what honouring looks like in observable terms, and — when a word cannot be kept — the explicit cleanup: acknowledge, make amends, give a revised word.

@context.description: JSON-LD context URI. Must resolve to the canonical integrity context.

@id.description: Canonical IRI for this integrity register. Pattern: agent:{agent_id}/integrity

legend.description: The legend or archetype embodied by this agent.

erhard_principle.description: First-person declaration of how Werner Erhard's definition of integrity applies to this role. Integrity is not moral virtue — it is the willingness to honour your word as yourself. This statement names what is at stake for this role when integrity is present or absent.

words_given.description: Ordered register of explicit words given by this agent towards implementation of ASI Saga and Business Infinity. Each entry is a WordGiven — a specific, first-person commitment with a clear by-when, observable honouring criteria, and a cleanup protocol for when the word cannot be kept.

WordGiven.title: WordGiven
WordGiven.description: A single word given — an explicit, first-person commitment authored through Werner Erhard's ontological integrity framework. A word given is not a wish or a plan; it is a declaration that binds the speaker to a specific outcome by a specific time. Being in integrity with this word means doing what you said, by when you said it. Being out of integrity means cleaning it up: acknowledge, make amends, give a revised word.

wordId.description: Unique identifier for this word given. Pattern: {ROLE}-WRD-{NNN}

name.description: Short label for this word (3–10 words), used as the Planner task title.

word.description: The explicit commitment in first-person form. Must begin with 'I will' or 'I give my word'. This is the word as given — not a description of work, but the declaration itself.

by_when.description: The specific completion target — an ISO 8601 date, a named milestone, or a relative condition. This is the temporal boundary of the word. Without a by-when there is no commitment, only a wish.

honoring_looks_like.description: Observable, verifiable evidence that this word has been honoured. What does 'done' look like to an outside observer? This is the measure of integrity — not effort or intention, but outcome.

word_status.description: Current integrity status of this word. 'honoring' — the word is active and being kept. 'complete' — the word has been fully honoured. 'out_of_integrity' — the word cannot be kept as given; cleanup is required.

cleanup.description: Populated only when word_status is 'out_of_integrity'. Documents the three steps of restoring integrity: acknowledge what was not honoured, make amends for the impact, and give a revised word with a new by-when.

planner_task_id.description: Microsoft Planner task ID once this word has been synced to a Planner plan. Populated by the planner sync workflow.

IntegrityCleanup.title: IntegrityCleanup
IntegrityCleanup.description: The three-step protocol for restoring integrity when a word cannot be kept: acknowledge, make amends, give a revised word.

acknowledged.description: Explicit statement of what word was given and could not be honoured. Named clearly, without deflection.

amends.description: What was done or will be done to address the impact of the broken word on those who were counting on it.

revised_word.description: The new commitment — restated in first-person form with clarity about what will be delivered.

revised_by_when.description: The new completion target for the revised word.
